helpful: Provisioned petition and brief-in-opposition full text — the BIO's vehicle arguments were decisive for calibrating below the naive CFR-conditional grant rate.
Statpack relist/CVSG cuts as a proxy for how court-initiated attention shifts grant odds.
gaps: No statpack cut for called-for-response (CFR) petitions — the strongest signal on this docket has no corpus-derived conditional base rate; had to rely on general background knowledge.

fedcourts query returned zero rows for citation-based filters (Purcell, Cooter & Gell) despite these being heavily-cited SCOTUS precedents; citation-overlap retrieval seems to have little coverage on the modern cert slice.
A cut for 'published dissent from denial of rehearing en banc below' would be valuable — it is a known cert signal with no corpus surface.
notes: Forward-mode cell with a same-day snapshot; live CourtListener retrieval added nothing, so the cell ran on provisioned inputs + statpack. Three corpus queries cost ~110 MB of ranged reads each and returned nothing — an expensive null.
